title: Nancy Yamamoto Oral History
creator: Tessaku
description: See this link for the most complete and recent information on this record: https://repository.californiarevealed.org/node/454961 .

Oral history interview with Nancy Yamamoto, who was a teenager living in Loomis at the time WWII broke out. Her aspirations to become a fashion designer were cut short with the signing of Executive Order 9066, and she was sent to Tule Lake concentration camp with her family. The family resettled in Berkeley, California after the war. Nearly 120,000 Japanese Americans were uprooted from their West Coast homes with the signing of Executive Order 9066 in February of 1942 and sent to inland concentration camps for four to five years.
